articles.title: How Guardiola found his best Man City XI
articles.summary: Manchester City are finishing the season strongly - having looked at one stage as if the Premier League title may elude them. Here's how Pep Guardiola went through many systems to find his best team.

articles.title: How Newcastle's 'bullies' became too nice
articles.summary: How Eddie Howe's Newcastle have gone from regularly ruffling feathers to losing 15 Premier League games this season.

articles.title: Could Spurs fans take solace from a season in the Championship?
articles.summary: Tottenham Hotspur fans will not want to contemplate relegation - but if the worst should happen, they may take solace from a season in the Championship.

articles.title: Arteta: Arsenal ready to 'get job done' and win title after post-Man City recharge
articles.summary: Mikel Arteta has urged his players to "take ownership" of the Premier League title race and "get the job done" after taking time to recharge and reflect following their loss to Manchester City.

articles.title: All eyes on Spurs & West Ham? 'Unbelievable' Forest edge towards safety
articles.summary: After Nottingham Forest's stunning win at Sunderland, is the Premier League relegation battle now a two-horse race?

articles.title: Rochdale and York set for 'unique' title decider
articles.summary: York City boss Stuart Maynard calls his side's final day National League title decider at Rochdale "one of the biggest games in the English football pyramid.

articles.title: Prestianni gets six-match ban for homophobic conduct
articles.summary: Gianluca Prestianni is given a six-game ban by Uefa for homophobic conduct during Benfica's Champions League knockout play-off match with Real Madrid in February.

articles.title: Why Wembley is special, for FA Cup semi-finals too
articles.summary: Tony Pulis explains why getting to Wembley is not just special for any manager, it is still hugely important for players, fans and all the staff at any club.

articles.title: Forest stun Sunderland to boost survival hopes
articles.summary: Nottingham Forest take a huge step towards Premier League survival, beating Sunderland 5-0 to go eight points clear of the bottom three.

articles.title: Dominant RB Leipzig see off Union Berlin
articles.summary: Goals from Max Finkgrafe, Romulo and Ridle Baku seal a comfortable win for RB Leipzig over Union Berlin, as they take three more points on their path to secure Champions League football next season.

articles.title: Hearts, Rangers, Celtic - who will lead Premiership by end of Sunday?
articles.summary: Three teams separated by three points with just five games to go. After an epic season, the Scottish Premiership split is finally here.

articles.title: Who will win the title? Reporters, fans and pundits make their predictions
articles.summary: Will it be Manchester City or Arsenal for the Premier League title? BBC Sport gets pundits, reporters and fans to predict each of their remaining fixtures - and who will win the league.

articles.title: Brobbey should have been sent off against Spurs - panel
articles.summary: Sunderland's Brian Brobbey should have been sent off for two yellow-card offences against Tottenham, a Premier League panel has ruled.

articles.title: Ex-Brighton boss Hughton reveals cancer diagnosis
articles.summary: Former Brighton and Newcastle manager Chris Hughton reveals he was treated for prostate cancer last year.

articles.title: 'I try to stay happy' - Trafford on tricky start to life at Man City
articles.summary: Manchester City goalkeeper James Trafford says he has not had "the best possible outcome" since joining the club.

highlights.title: Premier League highlights this week - Week Highlights

articles.title: Korda sets 36-hole scoring record to increase Chevron lead
articles.summary: Nelly Korda moved closer to a third major title as the world No 2 set the new 36-hole scoring record at the Chevron Championship to stretch her lead to six strokes heading into the weekend.

articles.title: West Brom docked two points for financial breaches
articles.summary: West Bromwich Albion are given a two-point penalty by the English Football League for breaching profit and sustainability rules.

articles.title: Leeds Rhinos thrash Catalans to remain top as Hull FC score 50 at Castleford
articles.summary: Leeds Rhinos ran in eight tries to demolish Catalans Dragons 46-4 at Headingley and remain top of Betfred Super League, while Hull FC won big at Castleford.

articles.title: Millwall snatch equaliser but automatic promotion hopes hit by draw at Leicester
articles.summary: Millwall moved back into the Championship's automatic promotion places but could only snatch a late 1-1 draw at already-relegated Leicester.

articles.title: Tongue takes five-for while Crawley's struggles continue for Kent
articles.summary: England fast bowler Josh Tongue claimed a five-for on the opening day of Nottinghamshire's County Championship clash with Warwickshire at Edgbaston.

articles.title: Korda sets 36-hole scoring record for Chevron Championship at -14
articles.summary: Nelly Korda talks through her second round after breaking the 36-hole scoring record for Chevron Championship at -14.

articles.title: Watch the goals and celebrations as St Johnstone clinch title
articles.summary: St Johnstone win 2-0 at Dunfermline Athletic to wrap up the Scottish Championship title with two games to spare.

highlights.title: Championship highlights this week - Week Highlights

articles.title: Rangers fans face ban for derby amid dispute with Celtic
articles.summary: Rangers ask the Scottish Professional Football League to step in after Celtic refused to give away fans any tickets to the final Old Firm derby of the season unless the Ibrox club's Union Bears ultras...
